article_title: Cancel ChatGPT Trends as Claude Hits Number One on App Store, Then Crashes Under Unprecedented Demand

content_preview.title: Cancel ChatGPT Trends as Claude Hits Number One on App Store, Then Crashes Under Unprecedented Demand
content_preview.summary: OpenAI's Pentagon deal sparked a consumer backlash that drove Anthropic's Claude to the top of Apple's App Store, before a surge in demand caused a worldwide outage.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

A geopolitical dispute between Anthropic and the U.S. Department of Defense has produced an unexpected market outcome: Anthropic's Claude AI chatbot climbed to the top of Apple's U.S. App Store free-apps chart on March 1, 2026, dethroning OpenAI's ChatGPT for the first time. The surge in downloads proved so large that Claude's infrastructure buckled under the strain the following morning, with Anthropic confirming a worldwide outage affecting Claude.ai, its mobile apps, and Claude C...

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-structured News article. The four-section format (Overview / What We Know / What We Don't Know / Analysis) is coherent and appropriate for this breaking-news story. Word count (885) fits comfortably within the 400–1200 range for News. Round 2 review: both required corrections from Round 1 have been applied correctly.
editor_notes.source_verification: All 6 sources verified. NPR timed out in the automated check but returns HTTP 200 on direct fetch — transient network issue. CNBC and both Fortune sources return 403 (bot-protection headers), which is standard anti-scraping behavior for those outlets; URLs are structurally valid and consistent with the story dates and topics. Al Jazeera and TechCrunch return HTTP 200. No blocklisted domains.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Round 1 finding 1 (sign-up growth metric) corrected: the body now reads 'daily sign-ups had quadrupled since the start of 2026', matching the Fortune source. Round 1 finding 2 (paid-subscriber claim) corrected: the claim has been removed entirely. The bot confirmed it was unverifiable against the cited sources. All remaining claims align with cited sources. The 'definitely' flag is a false positive — the word appears inside a properly attributed direct quote from Sam Altman, cited to Fortune.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional throughout. The analysis section correctly frames the structural tensions without editorializing. No loaded language introduced outside of properly attributed quotations.
editor_notes.originality: No duplicate or substantially overlapping articles found in the archive. No 2026-03 articles published yet; no 2026-02 articles address this specific chain of events.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Round 2 submission fully addresses both required corrections from Round 1. The article is factually sound, well-sourced, neutral in tone, and original. Approved for publication.
